selection_criteria: Prefer recognizable products or chemicals that regulators, product stewards, and scientists already understand without reading the code first.
Each goldset case must stress a nontrivial MCP behavior such as regional evidence reconciliation, near-field inhalation, PPE handling, or cross-MCP orchestration.
Each goldset case must carry at least one external source anchor so the showcase stays auditable.
Keep benchmark-regressed showcases separate from integration-only or challenge cases so the server never over-claims validation maturity.

cases.title: Hand cream direct-application loading realism
cases.why_it_matters: Direct hand application is common, easy to understand, and exposes whether the MCP is realistic about amount-per-area rather than only doing arithmetic correctly.
cases.showcase_story: Shows that the canonical dermal screening path is tied to a real workplace hand-cream application study rather than an arbitrary toy amount.
evidence_gaps: This anchor supports amount-per-area realism, but it does not validate retention or transfer behavior outside leave-on hand application.

external_sources.title: How much skin protection cream is actually applied in the workplace? Determination of dose per skin surface area in nurses

cases.title: Face cream SCCS guidance alignment
cases.why_it_matters: Face cream is a canonical cosmetic use case, and the MCP should be able to reproduce the official SCCS guidance values rather than only internal screening examples.
cases.showcase_story: Shows that the MCP can encode the SCCS Notes of Guidance face cream use pattern with the published daily amount, frequency, and application-area semantics kept explicit.
evidence_gaps: The SCCS tables anchor the use pattern, but they do not by themselves validate ingredient-specific transfer, dermal absorption, or product-specific formulation effects.

external_sources.title: SCCS Notes of Guidance for the Testing of Cosmetic Ingredients and their Safety Evaluation, 12th revision

cases.title: Trigger-spray cleaner aerosol burden
cases.why_it_matters: Cleaning sprays are widely recognized and expose the exact place where a naive room-average model can diverge from breathing-zone reality.
cases.showcase_story: Shows the value of the MCP's Tier 0 versus Tier 1 inhalation split against recognizable cleaning-spray literature, including both airborne-fraction realism and a narrow room-air decay half-life anchor.
evidence_gaps: The current solver still lacks raw executable chamber time series for direct calibration against the NF/FF implementation.

external_sources.title: Characterization of airborne particles from cleaning sprays and their corresponding respiratory deposition fractions
Quantitative assessment of inhalation exposure and deposited dose of aerosol from nanotechnology-based consumer sprays
Characterization of the aerosol release from spray cleaning and disinfection products - Spray scenarios in a climate chamber
external_sources.publisher: International Journal of Hygiene and Environmental Health

external_sources.title: Cleaning Products Fact Sheet

cases.title: Pediatric oral liquid delivered-dose realism
cases.why_it_matters: Liquid oral dosing is familiar to clinicians and parents, and it tests whether the MCP treats delivered dose as an exposure problem rather than as a perfect nominal label dose.
cases.showcase_story: Shows that the direct-oral path can be explained through a real dosing-accuracy study instead of a purely notional ingestion scenario.
evidence_gaps: The anchor is medication-specific and should not be over-generalized to all oral consumer or dietary scenarios.

external_sources.title: Liquid Medication Dosing Errors: Comparison of a Ready-to-Use Vigabatrin Solution to Reconstituted Solutions of Vigabatrin Powder for Oral Solution

cases.title: Janitorial disinfectant trigger spray with controls
cases.why_it_matters: This is a realistic occupational task that people immediately understand, and it stress-tests the handoff from screening to a control-aware worker execution path.
cases.showcase_story: Shows the MCP can route and execute a governed worker inhalation case, then exchange it through an ART-ready adapter contract without hiding its surrogate status.
evidence_gaps: The current execution kernel is ART-aligned and control-aware, but it is still not a native ART solver.

external_sources.title: Inhalation and dermal exposure to biocidal products during foam and spray applications
external_sources.publisher: Annals of Work Exposures and Health / BAuA

external_sources.title: Disinfectant Products Fact Sheet
Advanced REACH Tool (ART) official site

cases.title: Consumer disinfectant trigger spray vs Tier 1 inhalation benchmark
cases.why_it_matters: Disinfectant trigger sprays are highly recognizable and expose whether the Tier 1 NF/FF route can land inside a product-specific inhalation benchmark instead of only passing internal numeric fixtures.
cases.showcase_story: Shows that the MCP can reproduce a study-like consumer disinfectant trigger-spray task and match a narrow inhaled-dose band derived from the 2015 consumer spray supplementary dataset.
evidence_gaps: This is still a narrow product-specific inhaled-dose anchor rather than a full chamber time-series validation set for NF/FF sprays.

external_sources.title: Quantitative assessment of inhalation exposure and deposited dose of aerosol from nanotechnology-based consumer sprays (Supplementary Information)
Quantitative assessment of inhalation exposure and deposited dose of aerosol from nanotechnology-based consumer sprays

cases.title: Small-scale handheld biocidal spray vs occupational monitoring
cases.why_it_matters: This is a recognizable occupational spray task with measured personal-air data, so it demonstrates that the worker inhalation path is anchored to something more defensible than an internal numeric fixture.
cases.showcase_story: Shows the MCP can reproduce a study-like small-scale handheld BAC trigger-spray task and land inside the measured occupational breathing-zone concentration band from the 2023 biocidal spray monitoring paper.
evidence_gaps: The benchmark is still a narrow handheld BAC spray anchor rather than a broad worker inhalation calibration set.
The execution path remains ART-aligned and control-aware, but it is still not a native ART solver.

external_sources.title: Inhalation and dermal exposure to biocidal products during foam and spray applications
external_sources.publisher: Annals of Work Exposures and Health / BAuA

cases.title: Gloved wet-wipe dermal absorbed-dose screening
cases.why_it_matters: Wipe and cloth contact is easy to picture but difficult to model well because secondary transfer, PPE, and skin absorption all matter at once.
cases.showcase_story: Shows that the worker dermal path now executes a bounded absorbed-dose estimate rather than stopping at an external-contact bridge package.
evidence_gaps: The current dermal kernel uses bounded PPE and physchem modifiers, not full glove-breakthrough or chemical-specific permeation kinetics.

external_sources.title: Cleaning Products Fact Sheet

cases.title: Worker biocidal spray dermal-contact workflow
cases.why_it_matters: Spray disinfection work is common and easy to picture, but the dermal side is often ignored even though glove contamination, hand contact, and barrier assumptions all matter.
cases.showcase_story: Shows that the worker dermal path can now reproduce a study-like handheld BAC spray-contact task and land inside a narrow occupational dermal loading anchor instead of relying only on internal wet-wipe fixtures.
evidence_gaps: The current dermal execution kernel is still a bounded absorbed-dose model, not a glove-breakthrough or chemical-specific permeation solver.
The benchmark is still a narrow external skin-mass anchor rather than a full absorbed-dose calibration dataset.

external_sources.title: Inhalation and dermal exposure to biocidal products during foam and spray applications
external_sources.publisher: Annals of Work Exposures and Health / BAuA

external_sources.title: Disinfectant Products Fact Sheet

cases.title: Household mosquito aerosol room-air validation
cases.why_it_matters: Household insecticide aerosol use is easy to recognize and hard to model credibly because the whole-room airborne burden dominates the story.
cases.showcase_story: Shows that the air-space insecticide subtype is no longer just a heuristic branch in the defaults pack; it now has both a narrow executable room-air check and a sparse 0.75-hour to 6-hour decay series tied to published indoor aerosol measurements.
evidence_gaps: The current executable checks are still based on a narrow closed-room pyrethroid aerosol scenario, not a full family of measured insecticide aerosol chamber datasets.

external_sources.title: Exposures of infants and young children to pyrethroid pesticides in mosquito coils and indoor insecticide sprays

cases.title: EU Diazinon indoor surface insecticide workflow
cases.why_it_matters: Diazinon is a recognizable pesticide example that forces the MCP to reconcile chemical identity, regional product-use evidence, and subtype-specific exposure logic.
cases.showcase_story: Shows the end-to-end value of keeping CompTox and ConsExpo separate but interoperable: identity from EPA, EU use semantics from RIVM, then an auditable exposure scenario and PBPK-ready handoff.
evidence_gaps: The workflow is useful today, but it still lacks a pesticide-specific executable chamber or field benchmark for this exact consumer case.

Published indoor-surface insecticide monitoring studies for diazinon and chlorpyrifos mostly reflect broadcast treatment, treated-surface volatilization, and delayed reentry rather than the immediate trigger-spray cloud represented by the current screening kernel.

external_sources.title: EPA CompTox Chemicals Dashboard
Diazinon Technical Fact Sheet
Concentrations of diazinon, chlorpyrifos, and bendiocarb after application in offices
Potential exposure and health risks of infants following indoor residential pesticide applications
Pest Control Products Fact Sheet
ConsExpo Fact Sheets

cases.title: Air-space insecticide aerosol room-decay challenge
cases.why_it_matters: Room-filling insecticide aerosols are familiar and scientifically hard because they combine full-air release, room persistence, and deposition in a way that stresses a simple screening stack.
cases.showcase_story: This is the pesticide consumer case most likely to convince skeptical users that the MCP understands where it is still extrapolating and where a future validation package is needed.
evidence_gaps: Needs external chamber concentration time series and aerosol deposition anchors before it can move from challenge case to benchmark-regressed showcase.

external_sources.title: Pest Control Products Fact Sheet
ConsExpo

cases.title: Chlorpyrifos indoor-surface reentry benchmark
cases.why_it_matters: Chlorpyrifos is a recognizable indoor pesticide example with published post-application air data, and it now anchors the MCP's dedicated residual-air reentry path through both a reentry-start concentration benchmark and a sparse 4-hour to 24-hour room-air series.
cases.showcase_story: Shows that the MCP can benchmark a residual-air reentry scenario against published chlorpyrifos indoor air concentrations at reentry start and at the later 24-hour room-air point, while still making the remaining treated-surface emission gaps explicit.
evidence_gaps: The executable checks anchor reentry-start air concentration and one later room-air decay point, not full treated-surface emission, furnishing uptake, or home-specific decay behavior.
The published chlorpyrifos evidence is still a narrow broadcast-treatment analogue and should not be over-generalized to every indoor-surface insecticide scenario.

external_sources.title: Potential exposure and health risks of infants following indoor residential pesticide applications
Toxicological Profile for Chlorpyrifos
Revised Human Health Risk Assessment on Chlorpyrifos

cases.title: Diazinon office residual-air reentry benchmark
cases.why_it_matters: Diazinon is a recognizable organophosphate pesticide, and the office post-application measurements show how persistent indoor residual air can be even when the exposure starts well after application.
cases.showcase_story: Shows that the MCP can now benchmark a diazinon residual-air reentry scenario against a sparse one-day to two-day office-air series, without pretending that this is a full treated-surface emission model.
evidence_gaps: The executable checks anchor only a sparse office-air decay series, not furnishing uptake, source depletion, or worker-task determinants.
The source study is an office treatment analogue and should not be over-generalized to residential consumer trigger-spray cases.

external_sources.title: Concentrations of diazinon, chlorpyrifos, and bendiocarb after application in offices
Diazinon Technical Fact Sheet
